title: Kotlin Sealed Classes
content:
### Sealed Classes in Kotlin

Sealed classes represent restricted class hierarchies. All subclasses of a sealed class are known at compile time, enabling exhaustive `when` expressions.

---

### Declaring Sealed Classes

```kotlin
sealed class Result {
    data class Success(val data: Any) : Result()
    data class Error(val message: String) : Result()
    object Loading : Result()
}
```

---

### Sealed Class Constraints

| Platform | Subclass Location |
|----------|------------------|
| Kotlin/JVM | Same file (since Kotlin 1.9) |
| Kotlin/JVM before 1.9 | Same package and module |
| Kotlin/Multiplatform | Same source set |

---

### Exhaustive When Expressions

```kotlin
sealed class Status {
    object Active : Status()
    object Inactive : Status()
    data class Pending(val reason: String) : Status()
}

fun handleStatus(status: Status): String = when (status) {
    is Status.Active -> "User is active"
    is Status.Inactive -> "User is inactive"
    is Status.Pending -> "User is pending: ${status.reason}"
    // No else needed - all cases covered
}
```

---

### Practical Example: Network Result

```kotlin
sealed class NetworkResult<out T> {
    data class Success<T>(val data: T) : NetworkResult<T>()
    data class Error(val exception: Throwable) : NetworkResult<Nothing>()
    object Loading : NetworkResult<Nothing>()
}

fun <T> handleResult(result: NetworkResult<T>) = when (result) {
    is NetworkResult.Loading -> println("Loading...")
    is NetworkResult.Success -> println("Data: ${result.data}")
    is NetworkResult.Error -> println("Error: ${result.exception.message}")
}
```

---

### Sealed Interfaces

Kotlin also supports sealed interfaces.

```kotlin
sealed interface UiState {
    data class Loaded(val data: List<Any>) : UiState
    data class Error(val message: String) : UiState
    object Loading : UiState
}
```

---

### Sealed Class vs Enum

| Feature | Sealed Class | Enum |
|---------|-------------|------|
| Instances | Multiple (with different state) | Single per constant |
| Properties | Each subclass can have unique properties | Same properties for all |
| Subclass types | Class, data class, object | Only enum constants |

> **Key Point:** Sealed classes enable exhaustive `when` expressions. The compiler checks that all subclasses are covered, eliminating the need for an `else` branch.

quiz.question: Where must sealed class subclasses be defined?
What is a key advantage of sealed classes over enums?
options: Each subclass can have different properties
